posts.hook: By 2050, half the world will be nearsighted. We are in a myopia epidemic.
posts.caption:
In 1970: 25% of the world was myopic.
Today: 33%.
By 2050: 50% — that's 5 billion people.

This is the fastest-growing epidemic in eye health history.

The cause is clear: near work + reduced outdoor time.

The fix is also clear — if we act early.

Free tools: axeo.vision

#MyopiaEpidemic #EyeHealth #Axeo #HealthTok #VisionHealth #PublicHealth #ScreenTime

posts.hook: Presbyopia at 40 isn't inevitable. Here's what the research says.
posts.caption:
Presbyopia = the gradual loss of near-focus ability after 40.

It affects nearly everyone. But severity varies enormously.

Factors that make it worse:
- Low outdoor light exposure throughout life
- Chronic near work without breaks
- Poor nutrition (lutein, zeaxanthin deficiency)

Factors that help:
- Consistent accommodation exercises (near-far focus shifts)
- Outdoor activity — natural light keeps the ciliary muscle healthier

You can't stop aging. But you can slow the curve.

Exercises: axeo.vision/exercise

#Presbyopia #EyeHealth #Axeo #AxeoVision #AgingEyes #EyeExercise #Optometry

Both lines diverge clearly at age 40. Teal and amber lines. Clean medical chart aesthetic. 9:16. 28 seconds.

posts.hook: The global myopia epidemic — 3 stats that will change how you see screen time.
posts.caption:
The numbers are staggering.

📊 1 in 3 globally — myopic right now
📊 50% of the world — by 2050

And the #1 modifiable risk factor isn't genetics.

It's screen time replacing outdoor time.

posts.hook: Myopia vs hyperopia vs astigmatism — what's actually the difference?
posts.caption:
The 3 most common refractive errors — and how to tell them apart:

Myopia (nearsighted):
- Blurry at distance, clear up close
- Eyeball too long

Hyperopia (farsighted):
- Blurry up close, may be clear at distance
- Eyeball too short

Astigmatism:
- Blurry at ALL distances
- Cornea is oval, not round

You can have more than one.

Free awareness tests for all three: axeo.vision

#Myopia #Hyperopia #Astigmatism #EyeHealth #Axeo #Optometry #VisionTest

posts.hook: The myopia epidemic is the fastest-growing public health crisis you've never heard of. A thread:
posts.caption:
1/ By 2050, 50% of the world's population will be myopic. That's 5 billion people.

2/ In 1970, the global rate was 25%. Today it's 33%. We've added 8 percentage points in 50 years — and are about to add 17 more in 30.

3/ The cause isn't genetics — genetics haven't changed. It's behavior: less outdoor time, more sustained near work, starting younger.

4/ Natural light exposure is protective. Studies show 1 extra hour of outdoor time/day reduces myopia risk by 50% in children.

5/ The interventions exist. Atropine drops, orthokeratology, outdoor time, reduced near work. But they require awareness first.

6/ Axeo builds that awareness + provides exercises that support eye health: axeo.vision

Share if you learned something.
posts.visual_notes: Twitter/X thread. Data-driven. Shareable. Link at the end. Accessible to general audience.

posts.hook: I tracked my vision test results weekly for 30 days using a free browser tool — here's what I found
posts.caption:
r/EyeCare or r/Biohackers

Background: I've been mildly myopic for years (-1.25 / -1.50) and curious whether lifestyle changes could slow progression.

For 30 days I:
- Did the 20-20-20 rule strictly
- Added 1 hour of outdoor time daily
- Ran the free Snellen + contrast sensitivity tests weekly on axeo.vision

What I found:
- Acuity score stable (expected — 30 days is short)
- Contrast sensitivity improved by ~8% (surprising)
- Eye strain headaches: dropped from ~4/week to ~1/week
- Subjective: eyes feel less dry and tired by Friday

This isn't a clinical study. But the subjective changes were notable enough that I'm continuing.

The tool I used (axeo.vision) is free, no signup, browser-based — takes 4 minutes for all tests. Happy to answer questions about the protocol.
posts.visual_notes: First-person experience format. Authentic, data-driven, community-appropriate. Not promotional — shares personal protocol.
